objective: Run a controlled 100-code invitation beta with one unified 500-credit wallet; admit teachers in 10/20/30/40 batches and keep image generation explicit opt-in.

codePolicy.currentImplementationGap: BETA_MASTER_INVITE_CODES supports shared codes only; database-backed single-use code inventory is required before public distribution.

creditWallet.failurePolicy: Only successful operations settle; failed generation, timeout and failed image pages do not consume credits.
creditWallet.idempotency: Generation, version export, refinement and image settlement use stable operation references to prevent duplicate charges.

explicitlyOutOfScope: claims that all textbook editions are verified
commercial-ready claims before target-office and teacher-trial evidence

remainingExternalGates: cloud deployment, backup, monitoring and capacity testing

cohorts.activationStrategy: Invite in daily batches of 20-30; stop the same day if a hard stop is hit.
Add no more than 100 invitations per day after wave-100 promotion is signed off.
Add 200-250 invitations per day only after tested cloud capacity exceeds the configured admission limit.

entryPoints.placement: persistent header or lower-right utility action
entryPoints.label: 反馈问题

entryPoints.placement: generation status, result, export and version-history states
entryPoints.availability: when a project or task context exists
entryPoints.label: 反馈此课件

feedback.privacyRule: Do not request student names, phone numbers, IDs, scores or faces; redact secrets from logs and screenshots.

severityDefinitions.definition: Security/privacy exposure, data loss, corrupt downloads, or the primary generation/export path unavailable for 30% or more active users.
Generation/export failure for 10-30% of active users, severe textbook mismatch, or no workaround for a core subject.
Limited degradation below 10%, recoverable task failure, layout defect, or workaround available.
Cosmetic issue, low-impact copy issue, or feature request.

hardStopTriggers: confirmed data loss or artifact/hash mismatch
materially incorrect textbook identity affecting multiple teachers
rollbackProcedure: Pause new invitations and new generation admission.
Preserve running-job, database and artifact evidence; do not delete failed records.
Disable the affected feature or return to the last verified release.
Post an in-product incident notice and support-group update.
Verify login, upload, no-image generation, export, redownload and version reopen before resuming.
Resume at the previous cohort ceiling; never skip a cohort after rollback.

system.definition: completed no-image generation jobs / terminal no-image jobs
successful durable PPTX exports / export attempts
redownloaded artifacts with matching sha256 / sampled artifacts
sessions without unhandled client/server error / eligible sessions

product.definition: invited teachers who create one durable deck / accepted invitations
teachers reaching durable PPTX export / teachers starting material intake
structured classroom trials marked willing_to_reuse / submitted trials
teacher-rated decks usable directly or after no more than 15 minutes editing / rated decks
confirmed severity P0/P1 textbook mismatch / rated decks
feedback acknowledged within severity SLA / triaged feedback

universalNoGo: any known credential or privacy exposure
target-office acceptance not passed for the release candidate
cloud capacity test below the next cohort admission limit
promotionRequires: minimum cohort observation days and sample counts are met
all system and product targets are measured rather than assumed
no P0 and no P1 older than 24 hours
at least one real classroom trial in every active core subject before wave-1000
product, engineering, content QA and operations sign-off
goNoGo.decisionRecord: Record metric snapshot, incidents, unresolved feedback, owner signatures and release commit before every promotion.

teacherTrial.antiInflationRule: Workflow submission or automated review cannot award the five classroom-trial points.

prohibitedUploads: materials the teacher is not authorized to use
dataGovernance.modelTraining: No beta upload, generated deck or feedback is used for model training without a separate explicit opt-in.
dataGovernance.accessControl: Least privilege; production data access is logged and restricted to named operations and engineering responders.
dataGovernance.backup: Daily encrypted backup; restore drill before wave-100 and before every major release.
dataGovernance.exportAndDeletion: Teachers can request project export and account/project deletion through support during beta.

currentDecision.reason: The local RC and eight-subject paths are verified, but cloud deployment, target-office evidence, restore testing and real-teacher trials remain release gates.
currentDecision.nextGate: Deploy the invitation-only cloud environment, add operator RBAC/SLA queue and real quota enforcement, run backup/restore and capacity tests, then start wave-100.
